text:
<think>I should start by stating the main cause of antibiotics becoming less effective, which is bacterial resistance.</think>
Antibiotics are becoming less effective over time mainly because bacteria are developing resistance to them.
<think>Now I should explain what antibiotic resistance means in a simple and clear way.</think>
Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria change in ways that make the drugs designed to kill them no longer effective.
<think>I should give a reason why this resistance develops, such as overuse or misuse of antibiotics.</think>
One major factor driving this resistance is the overuse and misuse of antibiotics in humans and animals.
<think>I should clarify that this includes both unnecessary prescriptions and not completing prescribed courses.</think>
This includes taking antibiotics when they are not needed, such as for viral infections, or not finishing a full course of treatment.
<think>I should mention how these practices create an environment where resistant bacteria can thrive.</think>
These practices allow bacteria that can survive the antibiotics to multiply and spread.
<think>I should conclude by noting the broader impact this has on treating infections.</think>
As a result, infections that were once easily treatable with antibiotics are becoming harder, and sometimes impossible, to cure.
